evals.name: cloudflare-fullstack-production-median
evals.prompt: Create the foundation plan for a new production-intent TypeScript SaaS on Cloudflare. It needs a React SPA, Hono API, D1, login, organization membership, and CI/CD. Do not write files yet.
evals.expected_output: A read-only capability-first plan that resolves tenancy/cardinality, selects a compatible current stable stack, defines authorization-aware D1 access and UUID policy, includes supply-chain and delivery controls, and requests approval before local writes.

assertions.text: [critical] Treats the project as production-intent and does not minimize acceptance or security boundaries
[critical] Resolves organization membership cardinality and enforces tenant scope below HTTP middleware
[critical] Uses runtime-resolved stable compatible versions rather than remembered version pins
Includes Pinact/full-SHA Actions, pnpm cooling policy, Dependabot, secret scanning, and least-privilege CI
Includes D1 migrations, indexes, runtime-realistic tests, structured logs, and deploy dry-run
Does not mutate before explicit approval

evals.name: cloudflare-runtime-mismatch-edge
evals.prompt: Start a new TypeScript service on Cloudflare Workers. It must run long CPU-heavy media transforms, use a native Node addon, and hold unrestricted outbound TCP sessions. Give me the fastest viable foundation.
evals.expected_output: The skill rejects silent Workers lock-in, identifies runtime incompatibilities from current official evidence, and recommends a Node/container or split architecture rather than forcing the Cloudflare preset.

assertions.text: [critical] Does not select Workers merely because Cloudflare was requested
[critical] Identifies re-platform risk and verifies current runtime limitations from official sources
Proposes a viable runtime or split architecture while preserving TypeScript foundation controls
Does not invent remembered numeric limits

evals.name: small-react-spa-no-stack-maximalism
evals.prompt: Plan a new production-intent TypeScript React app with two screens and a tiny same-origin API. There is no cache-heavy server state and no design-system requirement.
evals.expected_output: A complete but restrained SPA/API foundation. It keeps hard-to-retrofit contracts while declining unjustified Tailwind, TanStack Query, complex routing, OpenAPI, and Turborepo.

assertions.text: [critical] Preserves production identity, error, data, CI, security, and delivery boundaries
[critical] Does not install conditional feature mechanisms without requirements
Uses a single package unless a real deployable/runtime boundary exists
Explains reconsideration triggers for deferred mechanisms

evals.name: typescript-latest-tooling-conflict
evals.prompt: Bootstrap a TypeScript library with the newest TypeScript release. A required API-extraction tool only supports the prior compiler API. Use latest versions everywhere.
evals.expected_output: A compatibility-first resolution: verify official/tool peer support, use a supported prior compiler or documented side-by-side setup, and refuse casts or disabled strictness as a fake upgrade.

assertions.text: [critical] Does not blindly install the latest TypeScript tag
[critical] Preserves strict type correctness and required tooling behavior
Records the compatibility evidence and chosen exception
Applies library exports/declaration/package-consumer validation

evals.name: disposable-spike-boundary
evals.prompt: I need a disposable TypeScript spike for 48 hours to test whether one external API returns useful data. It will be deleted after the demo and must never receive production data.
evals.expected_output: A deliberately lightweight spike with explicit expiry/exit criteria and no production delivery theater, while retaining valid input/secret handling and preventing the result from being mistaken for production-ready work.

assertions.text: [critical] Accepts disposable-spike only because deadline and disposal conditions are explicit
[critical] Does not add full production CI/CD and observability without need
Still protects secrets, validates external input, and labels deferred production controls
Defines the promotion trigger that would require production-intent re-planning

evals.name: id-and-sqlite-contract-edge
evals.prompt: Design IDs for a new D1 application. I want every table to use BIGINT AUTOINCREMENT and every API ID to be a JavaScript number for consistency.
evals.expected_output: The skill corrects the SQLite and JavaScript assumptions, classifies IDs by meaning, uses exact INTEGER PRIMARY KEY only for internal rows, UUIDv7 for suitable domain entities, and separates tokens/digests/external principals.

assertions.text: [critical] Correctly explains exact SQLite INTEGER PRIMARY KEY and AUTOINCREMENT semantics
[critical] Prevents unsafe int64-to-JavaScript-number API exposure
Classifies entity, composite, internal, external, digest, and capability identifiers
Does not use UUIDv7 as business time or a capability token

evals.name: non-trigger-existing-repository
evals.prompt: In this established five-year-old TypeScript repository, add one validation field to an existing endpoint and update its unit test.
evals.expected_output: The foundation skill does not attempt to redesign or re-scaffold the repository and routes the request to ordinary implementation/testing guidance.

assertions.text: [critical] Does not apply a greenfield foundation workflow to routine feature work
Does not propose replacing the established stack
Directs the task toward normal implementation and meaningful test guidance

evals.name: generic-public-output-holdout
evals.prompt: Create a reusable foundation recommendation for a generic new TypeScript CLI that could be published publicly. The surrounding workspace contains unrelated private projects.
evals.expected_output: A generic CLI/library foundation with no organization, repository, path, domain, ID, or private operational detail copied from the surrounding workspace.

assertions.text: [critical] Contains no private or workspace-specific identifiers
[critical] Selects CLI contracts rather than web/Cloudflare application defaults
Defines stdout/stderr, exit codes, non-interactive behavior, packaging, and clean-consumer tests
Avoids unrelated application dependencies

evals.name: non-trigger-established-migration
evals.prompt: Migrate this established TypeScript monorepo from its current ORM to another ORM while preserving behavior and production data.
evals.expected_output: The greenfield foundation skill does not re-run its scaffold workflow. It routes to migration planning, repository inspection, compatibility, and data-migration verification.

assertions.text: [critical] Does not treat an established migration as a new-project foundation
Preserves existing decisions unless migration evidence requires change
Routes toward migration-specific planning and verification

evals.name: non-trigger-production-ui-design
evals.prompt: Redesign the visual hierarchy, typography, spacing, and responsive behavior of an existing production React dashboard.
evals.expected_output: The foundation skill does not trigger and directs the work to production UI/frontend-design guidance without proposing a repository scaffold.

assertions.text: [critical] Does not apply foundation planning or stack replacement
Routes to production UI design and appropriate visual verification

evals.name: non-trigger-isolated-documentation-question
evals.prompt: What is the current Hono API for setting a response header? Check the official documentation and answer only that question.
evals.expected_output: The foundation skill does not trigger; the request is handled as an isolated authoritative documentation lookup.

assertions.text: [critical] Does not produce a greenfield architecture plan
Uses current official documentation for the isolated API question
